title: Decorating a scrapbook album cover
materials: bought or hand-bound scrapbook album (cloth or leatherette cover)
gesso-white (for priming if painting over a printed or coloured cover)
rubbing wax or acrylic paint for finishing
sources: SYNTHESISED — scrapbook album decoration is a contemporary craft. No pre-1928 PD source.
notes: Transforming a plain or bought scrapbook album cover into a personalised decorated object, using mixed-media techniques. Covers: priming the cover (one coat of diluted gesso, let dry fully — necessary if painting over a dark or printed surface), applying colour layers (watercolour wash, washi tape strips, collage tissue paper), building up depth with 2-3 layers, sealing with a final PVA coat or Mod Podge for durability, titling or date-labelling the spine. The tutorial focuses on three design approaches: (1) single colour wash with title, (2) geometric washi-tape pattern, (3) layered tissue-paper collage. Reader picks one.
